key_assumptions.source: Class A stockholders equity $340.3M; land largely in intangibles at acquisition cost — not a fair-value dhando floor for 315k+ acres

key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: Normalized consolidated owner cash of approximately $139M divided by 77.017M economic units; bracketed by FY2025 FCF of $120M and Q1 2026 annualized FCF of $164M.
Capital-light surface cash grows with the 2026 guide; options remain outside owner cash.

open_questions: Confirm Class A free cash flow attribution methodology vs distributions in Up-C structure
Related-party revenue fairness vs arm's-length benchmarks
Digital infrastructure pipeline: promote to base case only with SEC-filed contracts
Cross-holding overlap with **WBI** and **TPL** portfolio context
Alpha Digital lease economics when executed agreement appears in filings
thesis: LandBridge Company LLC owns and actively manages more than **315,000** surface acres in the Delaware Basin of West Texas and southeast New Mexico. **[Fact]** The company collects fee-based revenue from surface use royalties, easements, resource sales, resource royalties, and oil and gas royalties as operators and infrastructure companies develop land. **[Fact]** FY2025 consolidated revenue was **$199.1 million** (+81% versus 2024); consolidated net income **$72.4 million**; net income attributable to Class A shareholders **$30.1 million**; cash from operations **$126.3 million**; company-reported free cash flow **$120 million** (`8-K_20260225_exhibit_lb-ex99_1.htm`). **[Fact]** Q1 2026 revenue was **$51.0 million** (+16% year over year) with free cash flow **$40.9 million** and 2026 adjusted EBITDA guidance raised to **$210–230 million** (`8-K_20260506_exhibit_lb-ex99_1.htm`). **[Fact]** LandBridge IPO'd in June 2024 at **$17** per Class A share. Five Point Infrastructure / LandBridge Holdings retains control through Class B units and related-party agreements with **WaterBridge** (produced-water infrastructure; portfolio holding **WBI**). This is Permian land infrastructure, not an E&P operator.
why_market_wrong: The market may treat LandBridge as a high-growth land compounder after the post-IPO re-rating, or dismiss it as a sponsor-controlled fee stream with no asset floor. **[Inference]** At **~$78**, proof-first component economic value is **~$41 per share** while Lawrence base owner cash implies roughly **−2%** per year on normalized economic-unit cash flow. **[Fact]** GAAP book value for Class A holders (**~$12** per share) reflects acquired intangibles and retained earnings, not a mark-to-market of **315k+** Delaware acres. **[Inference]** Predictive attribute: **IPO / Up-C complexity** plus digital-infrastructure narrative that may run ahead of executed SEC contracts (Alpha Digital base option value remains **zero** in proofs).
